meaning: adj.公爵的,公爵领地的,象公爵的
examples: He asks you to meet him in the Ducal Citadel and runs off.
Ducal states at that time built walls to defend their own territories.
Stay in the Ducal Palace, Huo Yuhao knew, he could never get the soul ring.
From the late 13th century the ducal council began restricting political and economic rights, banning the commenda and nationalising trade.
As a clue to ducal solidarity, a shared outlook (16 dukes, for instance, went to one school, Eton) probably matters more than breeding.
